title: Junior recruiter (upto 15k / Target Based)
description:
Industry: HR & Recruitment Services — Talent Acquisition for startups, scale-ups, and SMBs across India. We deliver high-volume hiring, campus outreach, and candidate pipelines using digital sourcing and marketplace platforms.

Primary title (standardized): Recruitment Executive (Junior Recruiter)

This is a fully remote, target-driven role focused on sourcing, screening and placing entry-to-mid level talent. Compensation is fixed up to INR 15,000 with attractive performance-linked incentives.

Role & Responsibilities


Source candidates across job portals, LinkedIn, social media, and internal databases to build a steady talent pipeline.
Screen resumes and conduct structured initial calls to assess fit, salary expectations and availability.
Coordinate interview schedules between candidates and hiring managers; ensure timely feedback and follow-ups.
Manage candidate data and status updates in the ATS; maintain accurate records and pipeline metrics.
Achieve weekly/monthly sourcing and closure targets; proactively follow up to convert shortlisted candidates.
Support offer rollout and onboarding coordination, including documentation and basic negotiations under guidance.


Skills & Qualifications

Must-Have


Experience in high-volume or target-driven recruitment workflows.
Practical use of an ATS for pipeline management and status tracking.
Proficiency with LinkedIn sourcing and Boolean search techniques.
Comfortable using MS Excel for shortlisting, trackers and basic reports.
Clear verbal screening skills for candidate engagement and shortlisting.
Ability to work remotely, meet targets and manage time independently.


Preferred


Familiarity with LinkedIn Recruiter or Naukri recruiter tools.
Experience with CRM or recruitment platforms such as Zoho Recruit.
Knowledge of regional languages for localized sourcing across India.


Benefits & Culture Highlights


Fully remote and flexible work setup with performance-based incentives.
Fast-paced learning environment with on-the-job training in sourcing best practices.
Clear commission/bonus structure tied to closures and targets.


Join Career Bloc to build a career in recruitment — ideal for motivated, target-oriented individuals who want rapid skill growth and earnings upside.

title: Operations Executive - Transportation
description:
APM Terminals

Fulfillment & Logistics Operations Execution includes roles that manage and execute inventory, warehouse and fulfilment activities (planning and execution, distribution is covered by carrier management). Includes all kinds of warehouses like CFS, CY, inland depot / warehouses, bonded warehouses, etc. whether owned, operated or both by Maersk.

Administrative stream includes those who primarily support others by performing skilled technical, administrative or operational tasks. Jobs may range from highly supervised entry-level workers, technicians, and administrative staff to experienced individual contributors with robust technical or practical knowledge in specific areas, who have an ability to execute specialized work to support operations.

At the top of this career stream, individuals work independently and apply standards, yet can also make departures from established processes to resolve problems.

A colleague at this level works on tasks of limited scope and complexity, exercises independent judgment within defined boundaries and guidelines and typically under supervision. The colleague has skills developed through job-related training and on-the-job experience within a specific job discipline.

Colleagues have knowledge of standardized work routines and methods, general facts and information but may need to escalate non-routine problems to a more experienced colleague or supervisor.

The job requires limited job and business knowledge at the time of hiring.

Maersk is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace, and we embrace different styles of thinking. Maersk is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applicants without regard to race, colour, gender, sex, age, religion, creed, national origin, ancestry, citizenship, marital status, sexual orientation, physical or mental disability, medical condition, pregnancy or parental leave, veteran status, gender identity, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. We will consider qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with all legal requirements.

We are happy to support your need for any adjustments during the application and hiring process. If you need special assistance or an accommodation to use our website, apply for a position, or to perform a job, please contact us by emailing accommodationrequests@maersk.com.

title: Business Analyst - Apprentice
description:
APPRENTICE – BUSINESS ANALYST







MISSION

As a Business Analyst Apprentice, you will support the business analysis team in gathering requirements, analyzing data, and helping to design solutions that improve organizational processes. This entry-level role is designed to provide hands-on experience while you learn the fundamentals of business analysis in a structured environment.




OUTCOMES

Assist in gathering and documenting business requirements from stakeholders
Support in creating process maps, workflows, and use cases
Conduct basic data analysis to identify trends and insights
Help prepare reports, presentations, and documentation for management
Collaborate with Multiple Departments to validate solutions
Shadow senior analysts to learn best practices in requirement elicitation and stakeholder management
Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives within the organization




THE TEAM

Reporting into the Country Head of India and partnering with all the departments within the India Business.




THE SKILLS / EXPERTISE YOU BRING

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Basic understanding of business processes and IT systems
Good communication and interpersonal skills
Attention to detail and ability to work with structured documentation
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint)
Willingness to learn and adapt in a fast-paced environment




QUALIFICATIONS

Currently pursuing or recently completed a degree/diploma in Business, IT, Economics, or related field
Interest in business analysis, data, and process improvement
No prior experience required; training will be provided




BENEFITS OF APPRENTICESHIP

Structured training program with mentorship from experienced analysts
Exposure to real-world projects and stakeholders
Opportunity to develop technical and business skills
Potential pathway to a permanent Business Analyst role

description:

Data Entry Operator

title: Membership Consultant | Entry Level
description:
Job Summary;

We are seeking a highly motivated and results-driven Membership Consultant to join our sales team at Fitness First. As a Membership Consultant, you will be responsible for driving membership sales, achieving sales targets, and providing exceptional customer service to prospective members.




Responsibilities;

Sales and Revenue Growth:

1. Membership Sales: Generate new membership sales through prospecting, lead generation, and conversion of leads into sales.

2. Sales Targets: Achieve and exceed monthly and quarterly sales targets, contributing to the overall sales performance of the club.

3. Revenue Growth: Identify opportunities to upsell and cross-sell Fitness First services, including personal training, group fitness, and membership upgrades.




Sales Administration and Reporting:

1. Sales Administration: Manage sales administration tasks, including data entry, lead management, and sales reporting.

2. Sales Reporting: Provide regular sales reports to the Sales Manager, highlighting sales performance, and identifying areas for improvement.




Requirements;

1. Qualifications: Relevant qualifications in sales, marketing, or a related field.

2. Experience: Minimum 1-2 years of experience in sales, preferably in the fitness industry.

3. Communication Skills: Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage and persuade prospective members.

4. Sales Skills: Proven sales skills, with the ability to negotiate and close deals.

5. Results-Oriented: A results-driven approach, with a strong focus on achieving sales targets and driving revenue growth.

Show more
